name: xharvester
summary: ⚡ **xharvester v1.0** is a cross-platform enhanced Python-based reconnaissance and exploitation suite designed for security assessments of radio frequency (RF), wireless (Bluetooth & WiFi), industrial control systems (SCADA), and automotive systems with **automatic GitHub updates** and **full Android/Termux & IOS/a-Shell support**!

documentation.readme: ⚡ xharvester v1.0 is a cross-platform enhanced Python-based reconnaissance and exploitation suite designed for security assessments of radio frequency (RF), wireless (Bluetooth & WiFi), industrial control systems (SCADA), and automotive systems with automatic GitHub updates and full Android/Termux & IOS/a-Shell support!
usage_sections: Features: - 🔄 Auto-Update System: Option 99 now downloads latest version from GitHub automatically. - Preventing Attacks: Generate Security Report option gives a brief knowledage of rules to protect individuals against the threat module(Wi-Fi MODULE). - 🤖 Android/Termux Support: Full compa

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_reconnaissance_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Reconnaissance category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_reconnaissance_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Reconnaissance system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_reference_implementation_when_designing_a_new_v3_method_for_this_category
function_signatures.description: Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_teaching_example_in_the_prompt_stanza_so_the_llm_can_reason_about_the_right_run_order
function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for xharvester (n3twork5/xharvester). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for xharvester. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

examples: sudo apt install libdbus-1-dev libgirepository1.0-dev libcairo2-dev \
pkg install git python tsu aircrack-ng iptables hostapd dnsmasq -y

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with HarshitManhas/vulnerability-scanner-shodan (score=12); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with demonCoder95/wifi-recon (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ahirankush771/netprobe (score=11); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web, cloud, iots] in the Reconnaissance category. Fits when the engagement needs: ⚡ **xharvester v1.0** is a cross-platform enhanced Python-based reconnaissance and exploitation suite designed for security assessments of radio frequency (RF), wireless (Bluetooth & WiFi), industrial control systems (SC.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: ⚡ xharvester v1.0 is a cross-platform enhanced Python-based reconnaissance and exploitation suite designed for security assessments of radio frequency (RF), wireless (Bluetooth & WiFi), industrial control systems (SCADA), and automotive systems with automatic GitHub updates and full Android/Termux & IOS/a-Shell support!.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/n3twork5/xharvester.git; pip install -r requirements.txt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `python3 -m venv venv`. Further invocations from upstream: `source venv/bin/activate`; `python -m venv venv`. ⚡ xharvester v1.0 is a cross-platform enhanced Python-based reconnaissance and exploitation suite designed for security assessments of radio frequency (RF), wireless (Bluetooth & WiFi), industrial control systems (SCADA), and automotive systems with automatic GitHub updates and full Android/Termux & IOS/a-Shell support.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Reconnaissance, use xharvester because ⚡ **xharvester v1.0** is a cross-platform enhanced Python-based reconnaissance and exploitation suite designed for security assessments of radio frequency (RF),.
When the engagement is in the recon phase, use xharvester as a cataloged Reconnaissance tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python3 -m venv venv` as documented upstream for xharvester.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `source venv/bin/activate` as documented upstream for xharvester.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python -m venv venv` as documented upstream for xharvester.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo apt install libdbus-1-dev libgirepository1.0-dev libcairo2-dev \` as documented upstream for xharvester.
When chaining Reconnaissance tools, pair xharvester with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `xharvester --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: sudo apt install libdbus-1-dev libgirepository1.0-dev libcairo2-dev \
pkg install git python tsu aircrack-ng iptables hostapd dnsmasq -y
